title: Grade 4 Math
description: STAAR Grade 4 Math curriculum aligned to the Texas Essential Knowledge and Skills (TEKS). Topics are organized in teaching order from place value through personal financial literacy.

units.title: Place Value to 1,000,000,000
units.summary: Read, write, compare, and round whole numbers up to one billion.

lessons.title: Place value to one billion
lessons.objective: Represent the value of digits in whole numbers through 1,000,000,000.

questions.prompt: What is the value of the 6 in 4,628,135?
questions.explanation: The 6 sits in the hundred-thousands place: 6 × 100,000 = 600,000.

questions.prompt: Write 305,072 in expanded form's hundred-thousands value.
questions.explanation: The 3 is in the hundred-thousands place, so its value is 300,000.

lessons.title: Comparing & ordering
lessons.objective: Compare and order whole numbers to 1,000,000,000 using place value.

questions.prompt: Which list is in order from least to greatest?
questions.explanation: All three share 412, so compare the last three digits: 008 < 080 < 800.

lessons.title: Rounding to any place
lessons.objective: Round whole numbers to a given place.

questions.prompt: Round 36,847 to the nearest thousand.
questions.explanation: The hundreds digit is 8, which rounds the thousands digit up from 6 to 7.

units.title: Decimals to Hundredths
units.summary: Represent, compare, and relate decimals to hundredths and to fractions.

lessons.title: Decimal place value
lessons.objective: Represent decimals to the hundredths using money and concrete models.

questions.prompt: Which decimal is the same as 7 tenths and 3 hundredths?
questions.explanation: 7 tenths = 0.7, 3 hundredths = 0.03, total = 0.73.

lessons.title: Comparing decimals
lessons.objective: Compare and order decimals to hundredths using >, <, =.

questions.prompt: Which symbol makes this true?  0.4 ___ 0.39
questions.explanation: 0.4 = 0.40, and 0.40 > 0.39 because 40 hundredths is more than 39 hundredths.

lessons.title: Fractions and decimals
lessons.objective: Relate decimals to tenths and hundredths to fractions.

questions.prompt: Which fraction equals 0.45?
questions.explanation: 0.45 means 45 hundredths, written 45/100.

units.title: Fractions: Equivalence & Comparison
units.summary: Build, compare, and decompose fractions and mixed numbers.

lessons.title: Equivalent fractions
lessons.objective: Find equivalent fractions using multiplication or division.

questions.prompt: Which fraction is equivalent to 3/4?
questions.explanation: Multiply numerator and denominator by 3: 3×3/4×3 = 9/12.

lessons.title: Comparing fractions
lessons.objective: Compare two fractions with different numerators and denominators.

questions.prompt: Which fraction is greater than 2/3?

lessons.title: Decomposing fractions
lessons.objective: Decompose a fraction into a sum of unit fractions or simpler fractions.

questions.prompt: Which sum equals 5/8?
questions.explanation: 3/8 + 2/8 = 5/8. The other options sum to 3/8, 4/8, and 2/4.

units.title: Adding & Subtracting Fractions
units.summary: Add and subtract fractions with like denominators using objects, pictures, and properties.

lessons.title: Adding fractions (like denominators)
lessons.objective: Represent and solve addition of fractions with equal denominators.

questions.prompt: What is 3/8 + 2/8?
questions.explanation: Add the numerators and keep the denominator: 3+2 = 5, so 5/8.

lessons.title: Subtracting fractions (like denominators)
lessons.objective: Subtract fractions with the same denominator.

questions.explanation: Subtract the numerators: 7−3 = 4. Keep the denominator: 4/10.

units.title: Multi-Digit Multiplication
units.summary: Multiply up to a 4-digit by 1-digit and 2-digit by 2-digit numbers.

lessons.title: Multiplying by 1-digit
lessons.objective: Use the standard algorithm to multiply by a 1-digit number.

questions.prompt: What is 1,247 × 6?
questions.explanation: 6×7=42 (write 2 carry 4), 6×4+4=28 (write 8 carry 2), 6×2+2=14 (write 4 carry 1), 6×1+1=7.

lessons.title: Multiplying by 2-digit
lessons.objective: Multiply two 2-digit numbers using the standard algorithm.

questions.prompt: What is 38 × 24?

lessons.title: Multiplication word problems
lessons.objective: Solve real-world multiplication problems.

questions.prompt: A theater has 28 rows with 16 seats in each row. How many seats are there?

units.title: Division
units.summary: Divide up to 4-digit dividends by 1-digit divisors with and without remainders.

lessons.title: Long division basics
lessons.objective: Use the standard algorithm to divide.

questions.prompt: What is 392 ÷ 7?

lessons.title: Division with remainders
lessons.objective: Interpret remainders in division situations.

questions.prompt: There are 47 stickers shared equally among 5 students. How many stickers are left over?
questions.explanation: 47 ÷ 5 = 9 remainder 2. Each student gets 9 stickers and 2 are left.

units.title: Algebraic Patterns & Input-Output
units.summary: Represent multi-step problems with equations and identify number patterns.

lessons.title: Input-output tables
lessons.objective: Find a rule for input-output tables.

questions.prompt: If the rule is multiply by 4 then add 1, what is the output for input 6?

lessons.title: Multi-step equations
lessons.objective: Write an equation with a letter for the unknown.

questions.prompt: Maria bought 3 books for $8 each and a magazine for $4. Which equation finds her total t?
questions.explanation: 3 books at $8 = 3 × 8, then add the $4 magazine.

units.title: Geometry: Lines, Angles & Shapes
units.summary: Identify lines, classify angles, and measure with a protractor.

lessons.title: Lines & line segments
lessons.objective: Identify points, lines, line segments, rays, and parallel/perpendicular lines.

questions.prompt: Two lines that never cross and stay the same distance apart are called…
questions.explanation: Parallel lines never meet.

lessons.title: Classifying angles
lessons.objective: Identify acute, right, and obtuse angles.

questions.prompt: An angle that measures 130° is…
questions.explanation: Obtuse angles are between 90° and 180°.

lessons.title: Measuring with a protractor
lessons.objective: Measure angles with a protractor.

questions.prompt: An angle is half of a straight angle. How many degrees is it?
questions.explanation: A straight angle is 180°, so half is 90°.

units.title: Perimeter & Area
units.summary: Use formulas to find perimeter and area of rectangles and composite figures.

lessons.title: Perimeter of rectangles
lessons.objective: Calculate perimeter using P = 2L + 2W.

questions.prompt: A rectangle has length 9 cm and width 4 cm. What is its perimeter in cm?

lessons.title: Area of rectangles
lessons.objective: Calculate area using A = L × W.

questions.prompt: A rectangle has length 12 ft and width 5 ft. What is its area in square feet?
questions.explanation: A = 12 × 5 = 60 square feet.

units.title: Measurement Conversions
units.summary: Convert between units of length, weight, capacity, and time.

lessons.title: Customary length
lessons.objective: Convert between inches, feet, and yards.

questions.prompt: How many inches are in 4 feet?
questions.explanation: 1 foot = 12 inches, so 4 × 12 = 48 inches.

lessons.title: Time intervals
lessons.objective: Solve problems involving elapsed time.

questions.prompt: A movie starts at 1:45 p.m. and ends at 3:30 p.m. How long is the movie?
questions.explanation: From 1:45 to 3:00 is 1 h 15 min, plus 30 more min = 1 h 45 min.

units.title: Data Analysis
units.reportingCategory: Data Analysis and Personal Financial Literacy
units.summary: Represent and interpret data with frequency tables, dot plots, and stem-and-leaf plots.

lessons.title: Reading dot plots
lessons.objective: Interpret data displayed on a dot plot.

questions.prompt: On a dot plot of test scores, 4 dots stack at 90 and 2 dots stack at 100. How many students scored 90 or higher?
questions.explanation: Add the dots at 90 and at 100: 4 + 2 = 6 students.

units.title: Personal Financial Literacy
units.reportingCategory: Data Analysis and Personal Financial Literacy
units.summary: Distinguish income from expenses, fixed from variable expenses, and learn about saving and basic banking.

lessons.title: Fixed vs variable expenses
lessons.objective: Differentiate between fixed and variable expenses.

questions.prompt: Which is the BEST example of a fixed expense?
questions.explanation: Rent is the same amount each month, so it is a fixed expense.

lessons.title: Profit & income
lessons.objective: Describe profit as the result of subtracting expenses from income.

questions.prompt: A lemonade stand earns $42 and spends $15 on supplies. What is the profit in dollars?
questions.explanation: Profit = Income − Expenses = 42 − 15 = $27.
